items.text: apps/aion-dashboard exists as a workspace member with package.json declaring house scripts (dev, build, check, format, typecheck) matching apps/web.
The stack matches apps/web: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char line width); no alternate framework, styling system, or state library is introduced.
vite.config.ts builds a static SPA bundle and supports a configurable base path so AW can serve it under a sub-path.
src/main.tsx mounts the React root and index.html is the SPA entry; bun run build produces a static bundle and tsc --noEmit passes.
shadcn/Radix UI primitives are available under src/components/ui and used for all interactive controls.

Wire types (Event, WorkflowSummary, WorkflowStatus, WorkflowFilter, namespace) are sourced from the generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] → src/types/generated/), not hand-defined, and generated files are not hand-edited.
A typed REST client exposes listWorkflows/queryWorkflows (filter + pagination), getHistory(workflowId), and listNamespaces, each returning typed results.
The REST client returns typed errors and the not-yet-pinned AW contract details (paths, parameter names, pagination shape) are isolated in this one module.
Local-only view models (timeline entry, filter form state) are hand-written in feature types.ts and kept distinct from generated wire types.
A singleton WebSocket manager connects to the server's event stream and exposes subscribe-by-filter for per-workflow, filtered, and firehose subscriptions.
The manager parses incoming frames into typed Event values and dispatches them to registered handlers.
On disconnect the manager reconnects with bounded backoff and notifies connect/disconnect handlers driving a connection indicator.
On reconnect the active subscription is re-established and a resync is triggered (re-fetch or replay after last-seen sequence where the protocol supports it).
A useConnectionStatus hook exposes connected/reconnecting/disconnected state for the UI.
The list view loads workflow summaries via TanStack Query against the query API and renders them as rows (id, type, status, start/end time).
A filter bar filters by workflow type, status, and time range, driving the query.
The list paginates against the server's pagination contract and does not load full histories.
The list renders explicit loading (skeleton rows), empty (no workflows match this filter), and error (cause + retry) states.
A filtered WebSocket subscription updates rows live: status transitions update the corresponding row and newly-matching workflows are inserted.
Each row links to the workflow detail view.
The detail view loads a single workflow's full event history via TanStack Query against the history API.
Events project into a timeline model ordered strictly by event sequence number.

Each Event variant renders a timeline entry: lifecycle (started/completed/failed/cancelled), activity (scheduled/started/completed/failed), timer (started/fired/cancelled), signal (received), child workflow (started/completed/failed).
Activity events are correlated by ActivityId into one expandable group (retries shown as repeated failed attempts); timer events correlate by TimerId; child-workflow events correlate by child id.
Each entry shows its envelope (sequence number, recorded timestamp), a kind icon and colour, a one-line summary, and an expandable payload view.
Child-workflow entries link to the child workflow's own detail view.
The detail view renders explicit loading (skeleton timeline), empty (no events yet), and error (cause + retry) states.
A per-workflow WebSocket subscription appends live events to the timeline in sequence order as the execution progresses.
Live-appended events are de-duplicated by sequence number against events already loaded from the history fetch.
The timeline reflects the terminal outcome (completed/failed/cancelled/timed-out) when the closing lifecycle event arrives.
On socket drop the detail view resyncs (re-fetch history or replay after last-seen sequence) and the list view re-runs its query, with a reconnecting indicator visible throughout.
A firehose feed renders an unfiltered live event stream for observability.
A namespace context holds the selected namespace and a selector lets the operator switch it.
Every query and every subscription is scoped by the selected namespace; switching namespace re-scopes both the active query and the live subscription.
Shared StatusBadge, EventIcon, EmptyState, ErrorState (with retry), LoadingSkeleton, and ConnectionIndicator components exist and are reused across views.
The app shell wires routing (/ list, /workflows/:id detail) and the QueryClient, namespace, and websocket providers.
bun run check (Biome lint + format) and tsc --noEmit pass clean with no lint-disable comments added to silence findings.
